query:
Research the company "andersonsinc".

Research the company's recent financial performance and strategic initiatives: (1) Has there been a recent CEO change or new C-suite appointment? (2) Were Q3/Q4 earnings or guidance revised downward? (3) What is their revenue scale (Fortune 500, $10B+, public vs private)? (4) What is their primary industry (automotive, CPG, manufacturing, retail)? (5) What recent product launches or platform expansion is underway? (6) Are there any announced production capacity investments, pricing strategy shifts, or distribution expansion initiatives? (7) What is the gap between functional KPIs and total P&L performance based on earnings calls or analyst reports? (8) Are there any supply chain, demand forecasting, or margin compression challenges visible in recent filings or news?

Focus on the last 6 months. Return a comprehensive summary with specific facts, numbers, and dates.
answer:
**Executive Summary**
The Andersons, Inc. (NASDAQ: ANDE) has recently undergone a significant leadership transition, including the appointment of a new CEO, while executing an aggressive expansion strategy across its agribusiness and renewables segments. Despite recent top-line revenue misses in Q4 2025 and Q1 2026, the company has consistently beaten earnings expectations, driven by strategic tax credits and operational efficiency, leading management to raise its long-term earnings guidance rather than revise it downward. 

**Financial Performance and Scale**
*   **Revenue Scale and Industry:** The Andersons operates as a publicly traded company (NASDAQ: ANDE) primarily within the agribusiness, grain, ethanol, and fertilizer sectors [The Andersons, Inc. (NASDAQ:ANDE) Reports Q1 2026 Earnings Beat as Revenue Misses Estimates | ChartMill.com](https://www.chartmill.com/news/ANDE/Chartmill-47161-The-Andersons-Inc-NASDAQANDE-Reports-Q1-2026-Earnings-Beat-as-Revenue-Misses-Estimates). Based on recent quarterly revenues of $2.54 billion in Q4 2025 and $2.63 billion in Q1 2026, the company operates at an annualized revenue scale of approximately $10 billion [Andersons stock hits all-time high at 77.56 USD By Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/company-news/andersons-stock-hits-alltime-high-at-7756-usd-93CH-4645557); [Andersons Inc. (ANDE) Releases Q1 2026 Earnings: Revenue Down, EPS Beats | Quiver Quantitative](https://www.quiverquant.com/news/Andersons+Inc.+%28ANDE%29+Releases+Q1+2026+Earnings%3A+Revenue+Down%2C+EPS+Beats). 
*   **Earnings and Guidance Revisions:** The company has not revised guidance downward; conversely, it has raised its outlook. Management updated its long-range target to a $7.00 run-rate EPS by 2028 and expects to exit 2026 with a run-rate EPS higher than its previous $4.30 target [The Andersons ANDE Q4 2025 Earnings Transcript | The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2026/02/18/the-andersons-ande-q4-2025-earnings-transcript/). 
*   **KPI vs. P&L Gap:** There is a notable divergence between top-line revenue KPIs and bottom-line P&L performance. In Q4 2025, revenue missed the $3.28 billion consensus by coming in at $2.54 billion, yet adjusted EPS of $2.04 beat the $1.56 projection [Andersons stock hits all-time high at 77.56 USD By Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/company-news/andersons-stock-hits-alltime-high-at-7756-usd-93CH-4645557). Similarly, Q1 2026 revenue declined 1.2% year-over-year to $2.63 billion (missing the $2.76 billion estimate), but adjusted non-GAAP EPS of $1.12 substantially beat the $0.714 analyst projection [Andersons Inc. (ANDE) Releases Q1 2026 Earnings: Revenue Down, EPS Beats | Quiver Quantitative](https://www.quiverquant.com/news/Andersons+Inc.+%28ANDE%29+Releases+Q1+2026+Earnings%3A+Revenue+Down%2C+EPS+Beats); [The Andersons, Inc. (NASDAQ:ANDE) Reports Q1 2026 Earnings Beat as Revenue Misses Estimates | ChartMill.com](https://www.chartmill.com/news/ANDE/Chartmill-47161-The-Andersons-Inc-NASDAQANDE-Reports-Q1-2026-Earnings-Beat-as-Revenue-Misses-Estimates). This gap in Q1 2026 was partially bridged by the recognition of $26 million in 45Z producer tax credits [Andersons (ANDE) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript | The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2026/05/06/andersons-ande-q1-2026-earnings-transcript/).
*   **Margin Compression Challenges:** The company faced margin compression in its ethanol operations during Q1 2026, driven by a higher Eastern corn basis and elevated natural gas costs, which partially offset production gains [The Andersons, Inc. Q1 2026 Earnings Call Summary](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/andersons-inc-q1-2026-earnings-123000897.html). However, management noted that the grain cycle trough occurred in 2025 and expects market conditions to improve through 2026 [The Andersons, Inc. (NASDAQ:ANDE) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript - Insider Monkey](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/the-andersons-inc-nasdaqande-q1-2026-earnings-call-transcript-1755847/).

**Strategic Initiatives and Investments**
*   **Production Capacity Investments:** The Andersons is executing major capital expenditures, including a $70 million investment to expand its export terminal at the Port of Houston (expected completion in Q2 2026) to add export capacity for soybean meal and improve grain operation efficiency [The Andersons ANDE Q4 2025 Earnings Transcript | The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2026/02/18/the-andersons-ande-q4-2025-earnings-transcript/); [The Andersons Reports Third Quarter 2025 Results with Strong Renewables Performance and Strategic Growth Plans](https://igrownews.com/the-andersons-inc-latest-news/). Additionally, the company is investing $60 million to expand ethanol production capacity at its Clymers, Indiana plant by 30 million gallons per year (MMgy), targeting 170 MMgy by 2027 [The Andersons to boost capacity at Indiana plant to 170 MMgy | Ethanol Producer Magazine](https://ethanolproducer.com/articles/the-andersons-to-boost-capacity-at-indiana-plant-to-170-mmgy).
*   **Platform Expansion and M&A:** The company completed a $425 million acquisition to secure 100% ownership of its ethanol facilities [The Andersons sets ambition growth target for 2028 | World Grain](https://www.world-grain.com/articles/22180-the-andersons-sets-ambition-growth-target-for-2028). It also acquired the assets of Skyland Grain, LLC, adding over 40 facilities and 100 million bushels of storage capacity across Kansas, Colorado, and Texas [The Andersons reveals 2028 growth target | Baking Business](https://www.bakingbusiness.com/articles/65347-the-andersons-reveals-2028-growth-target]. Recent acquisitions also include Reed & Perrine and ACJ International [List of 11 Acquisitions by The Andersons (Apr 2025) - Tracxn](https://tracxn.com/d/acquisitions/acquisitions-by-the-andersons/__5Q4z40SPRcnw28NGfxLqIyW7ToCzj-aY-eixflXGEHM).
*   **Distribution and Product Initiatives:** The company entered a distribution agreement with Phospholutions Inc. for the 2026 crop year to distribute RhizoSorb fertilizer technology, supported by an investment from its venture capital arm, Maumee Ventures [Phospholutions Expands Distribution Through Partnership with The Andersons - CropLife](https://www.croplife.com/crop-inputs/fertilizer/phospholutions-expands-distribution-through-partnership-with-the-andersons/). The company is also actively expanding its premium food corn operations to meet rising customer demand [The Andersons Reports Third Quarter 2025 Results with Strong Renewables Performance and Strategic Growth Plans](https://igrownews.com/the-andersons-inc-latest-news/).

**Leadership and C-Suite Transitions**
The Andersons has completely overhauled its executive leadership team over the past year:
*   **CEO Transition:** Bill Krueger succeeded Pat Bowe as President and CEO on October 1, 2024, with Bowe transitioning to Executive Chairman of the Board [The Andersons, Inc. Names Bill Krueger as CEO Effective October 1, Pat Bowe Will Remain Chairman](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/the-andersons-inc-names-bill-krueger-as-ceo-effective-october-1-pat-bowe-will-remain-chairman-302224559.html); [The Andersons, Inc. 2025 Proxy Statement](https://filecache.investorroom.com/mr5ir_andersonsinc/454/download/2025%20Proxy%20Statement.pdf).
*   **Segment Leadership:** Effective January 1, 2025, Weston Heide was appointed Executive Vice President (EVP) of Agribusiness, and Mark Simmons was appointed EVP of Renewables [The Andersons, Inc. Appoints Executive Vice Presidents to Lead Agribusiness and Renewables Segments](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/the-andersons-inc-appoints-executive-vice-presidents-to-lead-agribusiness-and-renewables-segments-302328166.html). Joseph E. McNeely retired as President of Nutrient and Industrial on December 31, 2024 [The Andersons, Inc. 2025 Proxy Statement](https://filecache.investorroom.com/mr5ir_andersonsinc/454/download/2025%20Proxy%20Statement.pdf).
*   **Legal and Corporate:** Emmanuel Ayuk was appointed EVP, General Counsel, and Corporate Secretary on September 2, 2025, succeeding Christine M. Castellano, who retired on April 18, 2025 [The Andersons Appoints Emmanuel Ayuk as Executive Vice President, General Counsel, and Corporate Secretary](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/the-andersons-appoints-emmanuel-ayuk-as-executive-vice-president-general-counsel-and-corporate-secretary-302530431.html); [Andersons Inc. announces executive retirement By Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/sec-filings/andersons-inc-announces-executive-retirement-93CH-3950509).
*   **Other Key Appointments:** In 2025, Brian K. Walz became SVP and Treasurer, and Anne G. Rex became SVP of Strategy, Planning and Development [The Andersons, Inc. 2025 Proxy Statement](https://filecache.investorroom.com/mr5ir_andersonsinc/454/download/2025%20Proxy%20Statement.pdf). Steven Oakland joined the Board of Directors on August 21, 2025 [The Andersons, Inc. Names Steven Oakland to Board of Directors](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/the-andersons-inc-names-steven-oakland-to-board-of-directors-302536009.html).

**Gaps and Limitations**
*   **Data Exclusion:** The provided research findings included financial data for "Andersen Group (ANDG)". Because the prompt specifically requested research on "andersonsinc" (The Andersons, Inc. / NASDAQ: ANDE), all data pertaining to Andersen Group (ANDG) was excluded from this report to prevent inaccurate cross-contamination of corporate data.
*   **Missing Information:** The findings do not explicitly state whether The Andersons is listed on the Fortune 500. Additionally, there is no specific mention of explicit pricing strategy shifts or demand forecasting challenges, aside from the noted margin pressures from corn basis and natural gas costs.
